phases.title: Announcement of Schedule
phases.description: The Election Commission of India announces the election dates and the Model Code of Conduct comes into force.

phases.title: Filing of Nominations
phases.description: Candidates file their nomination papers, which are scrutinized by the Election Commission.

phases.title: Campaigning
phases.description: Parties and candidates campaign across the country. Campaigning stops 48 hours before voting.

phases.title: Polling Day(s)
phases.description: Voters cast their votes using Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs). Due to India's size, this often happens in multiple phases.

phases.title: Counting and Results
phases.description: Votes are counted and results are declared. The party or coalition with a majority in the Lok Sabha forms the government.
phases.timeline: A specific day after all polling phases conclude
